message.content:
Test file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_13-03-01_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7/src/claim-office.spec.ts
Failing test: "should apply 20% loyalty discount for customer with exactly 2 years with MHPCO"
Expected: 85 G premium for plain sword + 2-year customer
Current error: Expected { premium: 85 }, received { premium: 105 }
Implementation file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_13-03-01_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7/src/claim-office.ts

Add policy-wide modifier: if customer.yearsWithMHPCO >= 2, subtract 20% of policy base premium.

Per spec: policy-wide modifiers apply to the SUM of item base premiums (not surcharged premiums). Looking at integration example: "100 G base + 50 G curse + 30 G high enchantment − 20 G loyalty" - the 20G loyalty = 20% of 100G base. So loyalty is 20% of POLICY BASE PREMIUM (sum of item base premiums BEFORE item-specific surcharges).

Implementation: compute base premium sum, item surcharges (added to base), and policy-wide adjustments (computed from base sum). Final: base + item-surcharges + policy-wide-adjustments + fee.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text: I'll analyze the failing test and implement the minimal code needed to make it pass.
Now I'll implement the minimal change to make the failing test pass. The test expects 85 G for a plain sword with a 2-year customer (100 base − 20 loyalty + 5 fee = 85).

content.text:
All tests pass. 17 tests passing including the new loyalty discount test.

Green Phase Complete:
- **Implementation**: Added `loyaltyAdjustment` that applies a -20% discount on policy base premium when `customer.yearsWithMHPCO >= 2`. Introduced `nonComponentsBasePremium` to compute the base sum (without item-specific surcharges). Updated `quote` to compose: items-total (with surcharges) + policy-wide-adjustments (from base sum) + processing fee.
- **Result**: All 17 tests pass (35 todo).
- **Approach**: Minimal — only the loyalty modifier is wired in. The structure separates "base sum" from "surcharged sum" as the spec requires, but no other policy modifiers (first insurance, follow-up) are implemented yet — those will come in subsequent Red phases.

Implementation file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_13-03-01_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7/src/claim-office.ts

Proceeding to Refactor phase.
